topic: help with iPad — can't figure out updates

turns.content: I am in need of some technical assistance, if you would not mind. My iPad is insisting that I update it, and I confess I am not entirely sure how to proceed.

Of course, I'm happy to help with that. iPads do prompt you to update periodically, and it's generally a good idea to keep them current. What exactly are you seeing on the screen? Is it a notification that popped up, or did you go into the settings yourself?

A notification appeared this morning. Something about iPadOS and a number — 18, I believe. It has a button that says "Details" and another that says "Later." I pressed "Later" the last three times it appeared, but it keeps coming back, rather insistently.

Yes, that would be iPadOS 18 — it's a major software update. Apple can be quite persistent with those notifications! It's worth doing, as it includes security fixes and new features. Here's how to go about it:

1. First, make sure your iPad is **connected to Wi-Fi** — updates won't download over mobile data
2. **Plug it into its charger** — the iPad needs to be charging or have at least 50% battery to install an update
3. Then go to **Settings** (the grey gear icon) > **General** > **Software Update**

Can you get to that screen and tell me what it shows?

Right. Let me find my glasses. One moment. Yes, I have the Settings open. General. Software Update. It says "iPadOS 18.3.2" and there is a blue button that says "Download and Install." Underneath it says something about 2.1 gigabytes. Is that a great deal?

2.1 gigabytes is a moderate size for a major update — it's not unusual. On a typical home broadband connection, the download should take somewhere between 10 and 30 minutes, depending on your internet speed. The installation after that takes another 15-20 minutes, during which the iPad will restart itself and show an Apple logo with a progress bar.

Before you tap that button, two things to check:
- Is your iPad plugged into its charger?
- Are you on your home Wi-Fi?

If both are yes, you're safe to tap "Download and Install." It will ask for your passcode — the number you type in to unlock the iPad.

It is on the charger, yes, and I am on the Wi-Fi. The passcode — that would be the six-digit number? I do know that one. Let me tap the button. It is asking for the passcode now. I shall enter it. Right. It now says "Downloading" with a small progress indicator. I should think it will take a while on my connection.

Perfect, you've done everything correctly. Just let it download — you can continue using the iPad while it downloads, but I'd suggest staying on Wi-Fi and keeping it on the charger. Once the download finishes, it will ask if you want to install now or install tonight. If you tap "Install Now," the iPad will go dark for a bit and restart with the Apple logo and a progress bar. That's all completely normal — just leave it alone and let it do its thing. It should be finished within about 20 minutes of starting the installation.
And I shan't lose anything? Photographs, my notes, the video calling application? My daughter set that up for me and I would rather not have to ask her to do it again.

No, you won't lose anything at all. A software update keeps all your apps, photos, notes, and settings exactly where they are. It only updates the underlying operating system. Your video calling app, your photos, everything will be right where you left it. The iPad might look very slightly different after the update — Apple sometimes moves a button here or there — but all your content and apps will be intact. You won't need to set anything up again.

That is a relief. I must say, I find these things rather daunting. In my working days one had a secretary who dealt with the computers. Now it is just me and this tablet, and we do not always see eye to eye. Thank you for your patience.

You're very welcome, and there's nothing daunting about it — you've handled this perfectly. Once the update finishes and the iPad restarts, just unlock it as normal and check that everything looks right. If anything seems odd or if it asks you any questions you're unsure about after the restart, don't hesitate to ask. Enjoy your newly updated iPad.
